fighters.name: Brian Tyler Cohen
fighters.description: Political commentator breaking down the news with clarity and urgency. Host of No Lie with Brian Tyler Cohen.

fighters.name: Glenn Kirschner
fighters.description: Former 30-year federal prosecutor. Host of Justice Matters — daily legal analysis connecting the dots on accountability.

fighters.name: Al Franken
fighters.description: Former U.S. Senator, author, and podcast host. Political satire and commentary rooted in policy substance.

fighters.name: Joe Trippi
fighters.description: Veteran Democratic strategist and political consultant. Host of That Trippi Show.

fighters.name: David Pepper
fighters.description: Democracy advocate, author, and former Ohio Democratic Party chair. Writes Pepperspectives.

fighters.name: Reed Galen
fighters.description: Lincoln Project co-founder and political strategist. Writes The Home Front.

fighters.name: Rick Wilson
fighters.description: Lincoln Project co-founder, bestselling author, and Republican strategist turned pro-democracy advocate.

fighters.name: #SistersInLaw
fighters.description: Webby Award-winning podcast with Joyce Vance, Jill Wine-Banks, Barb McQuade, and Kimberly Atkins Stohr.

fighters.name: Simon Rosenberg
fighters.description: Democratic strategist who called the 2022 red wave bluff. Writes the Hopium Chronicles — data over doom.

fighters.name: Inequality Media
fighters.description: Robert Reich's media organization producing economic explainers that cut through political noise with data and history.

fighters.name: Kimberly Wehle
fighters.description: Constitutional law professor, author, and legal analyst translating the machinery of government into language everyone can use.

fighters.name: John Della Volpe
fighters.description: Director of polling at the Harvard Institute of Politics. Author and expert on youth civic engagement.

fighters.name: Joyce Vance
fighters.description: Former U.S. Attorney for the Northern District of Alabama. Legal analyst, law professor, and civil rights voice.

fighters.name: The Contrarian
fighters.description: Jennifer Rubin and Norman Eisen's pro-democracy news organization. Unflinching journalism — truthful, not neutral.

fighters.name: The Union
fighters.description: Pro-democracy grassroots coalition. 192,000+ members across all 50 states with 100+ partner organizations.

fighters.name: The Lincoln Project
fighters.description: Anti-authoritarian political action committee. Republicans and independents defending democratic norms.

fighters.name: Katie Phang
fighters.description: Trial attorney and legal analyst. Formerly MSNBC, now delivering daily legal breakdowns on YouTube.

fighters.name: Democracy Docket
fighters.description: Marc Elias's voting rights platform. Tracking 1,000+ election law cases.

fighters.name: Crimson Goes Blue
fighters.description: Harvard alumni-led organization mobilizing graduates for pro-democracy organizing and 2026 midterm engagement.

fighters.name: Heather Cox Richardson
fighters.description: Historian, Boston College professor, and author of Letters from an American. 2.9M subscribers.

fighters.name: Harry Litman
fighters.description: Former U.S. Attorney and Deputy Assistant AG. Creator of Talking Feds — roundtable legal analysis.
